SHERIFF DEPUTY REPORT. TREE PULLED UP. DOWNED POWER LINES. DUNROVEN RD AND BENNOCK MILL RD. (REPORTED AS TORNADO, UNCONFIRMED). TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE TOWN OF MCBEAN NEAR THE BORDER OF RICHMOND AND BURKE COUNTIES. THERE WERE SEVERAL HOMES DAMAGED NEAR DONE ROVEN ROAD AND BENNOCK MILL ROAD. A FIFTH WHEEL TRAVEL TRAILER WAS OVERTURNED AND SEVERELY DAMAGED. THERE WAS ALSO SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE IN THE AREA.

PUBLIC REPORTED TREES DOWN ON ORIOLE ST IN AIKEN. REPORT AND PHOTOS RELAYED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED.

SURVEY FOUND AN EF-2 TORNADO DAMAGE IN THE WOODSIDE PLANTATION SUBDIVISION AND ALSO AIKEN ESTATES SUBDIVISION. WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE AT 115 MPH. THERE WAS SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE IN THE AREA ALONG WITH DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOME.
SC HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED TREES IN THE ROADWAY AT THE INTERSECTION OF SILVER BLUFF RD AND DOUGHERTY RD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

STRONG STRAIGHT LINE WINDS PRODUCED SOME DAMAGE OF A HOME JUST OFF OF BULL SWAMP ROAD. THE STRONG WINDS DAMAGED THE ROOF OF THE HOME AND ALSO BLEW OUT A WALL ON THE NE SIDE OF THE HOUSE. THERE WERE ALSO SOME MEDIUM SIZE LIMBS DOWN AROUND THE HOME. NEARBY ALONG FRINGETREE ROAD WINDS TOPPLED TREES AND POWERLINES.
TREES DOWN ON PLANTATION DRIVE

*** 2 INJ *** AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR SUNNY PLAIN ROAD AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST ACROSS OTT SISTERS ROAD THEN WEAKENED AS IT CROSSED I-26. WINDS WERE DETERMINED TO RANGE FROM 105 MPH TO 110 MPH. A MOBILE HOME WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED. THERE WERE 7 HOMES DAMAGED ALONG WITH 6 OUT BUILDINGS DAMAGED. THE LENGTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 1 MILE WITH A WIDTH OF 200 YARDS AT ITS WIDEST SECTION. THERE WERE NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED AND A FEW HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES SNAPPED OFF APPROXIMATELY 10 TO 20 FEET OFF THE GROUND. A TREE FELL ON A MOBILE HOME. TORNADO DURATION 5 MINUTES.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONDUCTED A STORM SURVEY IN EDGEFIELD COUNTY AND FOUND A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN APPROXIMATELY 3.5 MILES EAST OF EDGEFIELD TO 1.5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF JOHNSTON. RESULTS DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO INTERMITTENTLY TOUCHED DOWN NEAR TWO MILE ROAD AND MOVED NORTHEAST TO NEAR BLAND BAPTIST ROAD. NO DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES WERE OBSERVED...ONLY TREES DOWN. WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 80 MPH AND THE PATH WAS ABOUT 3.5 MILES IN LENGTH AND ABOUT 50 YARDS WIDE. TORNADO DURATION 5 MINUTES.
EDGEFIELD COUNTY DISPATCH REPORTED TREES DOWN AT VARIOUS LOCATIONS IN THE TOWN OF EDGEFIELD. INCLUDING TREES DOWN AT THE INTERSECTION OF BOUKNIGHT RD AND WOODYARD RD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONDUCTED A STORM SURVEY IN LEXINGTON COUNTY AND FOUND A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF LAKE MURRAY AND THEN AGAIN ON THE NORTH SHORE NEAR BALENTINE. THE INITIAL TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED ALONG ST. JOHN CHURCH ROAD AND TURNER CIRCLE WITH THE LAST DAMAGE OBSERVED ON SHADOWOOD DRIVE ON THE NORTH SHORE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWN ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE LAKE ALONG WITH MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES AND A FEW TREES WERE DOWN ON A HOME ON THE NORTH SIDE. A PONTOON BOAT WAS ALSOVLIFTED OUT OF THE WATER AND DEPOSITED SIDEWAYS ON THE END OF A DOCK. RESULTS DETERMINED IT WAS AN EF1 TORNADO WITH WINDS AROUND 95-100 MPH AT THE INITIAL TOUCHDOWN. THE LENGTH OF THE PATH WAS ABOUT 9.5 MILES AND 200 YARDS WIDE. TORNADO DURATION 13 MINUTES.
